behavior_summary: DEADNS::look: When a player/actor examines the dead-body item, the item sets processing context (info/process-exclude), then emits the short description string "dead body " directed at the inspecting process and suspends. This is a short, purely descriptive 'U8P_DEADNS::look' handler.

DEADNS::use: When a player/actor 'uses' (activates) the dead-body item the script reads the item's status word and tests bit 0x0100. If that bit is clear the item closes any related UI/gump and returns. If the bit is set, the script makes a random roll (urandom(100)) and on a rare outcome (roll > 95 inferred) the item barks "leave my bones alone... " to the player and the script suspends; otherwise it opens a UI gump with id 0x0C.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DEADNS::DEADNS::look: linear handler — set info/process-exclude, call Item::bark(pid, "dead body "), do housekeeping (implies/pop), suspend/return. Confidence: high.
U8P_DEADNS::DEADNS::use: read item status via Item::getStatus(); test status & 0x0100. If bit 0x0100 == 0 -> call Item::closeGump() and return. Confidence: high.

U8P_DEADNS::DEADNS::use: if status & 0x0100 != 0 -> call urandom(100). If urandom > 95 (small chance) -> call Item::bark(pid, "leave my bones alone... ") and suspend; else call Item::openGump(0x0C). Confidence: medium (random threshold and urandom range inferred).
open_questions: What is the semantic meaning of the 0x0100 status bit on the item (enables UI interactions, ownership, or other state)? Confidence: low.
Does urandom(n) return 0..n-1 exactly, and therefore is the >95 comparison equivalent to a ~4% chance? This is inferred from usage but not explicit. Confidence: medium.
Are the bark strings intentionally containing trailing spaces ("dead body ", "leave my bones alone... ") or is that an artifact of extraction? Confidence: low.
What exact downstream effects does the set-info / process-exclude sequence have for this item beyond preventing further processing? Confidence: medium.

main_function.name: U8P_DEADNS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive response when the dead-body item is examined by an actor: set per-process/info context and emit the text "dead body " to the inspecting process, then perform suspension/housekeeping.
entry_conditions: Triggered when an actor/player looks at (examines) the item — the item's U8P_DEADNS::look/observe area is entered.

function_map.name: U8P_DEADNS::look
function_map.role: Examination/U8P_DEADNS::look handler that emits a descriptive bark to the inspecting process.

function_map.name: U8P_DEADNS::use
function_map.role: Use/activate handler: checks an item status bit to decide whether to close UI, open a gump, or occasionally produce a refusal bark and suspend.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided utility: display/emit a short text message from the item to a process/actor.

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Return item's status word (bitflags) used by the script to branch on 0x0100.

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Close UI/gump associated with the item; used when the status bit is clear.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Pseudo-random number generator; called with 100 to decide a rare special-case branch.

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Open a UI/gump for the item (called with id 0x0C on the normal enabled path).

external_calls.purpose: Emit the short description string "dead body " to the inspecting process (pid) when the item is examined.
Fetch the item's status bitfield so the script can test bit 0x0100 to determine permitted interactions.
Close any open UI/gump when the enabling status bit (0x0100) is not set, then return.
Make a random roll (called with 100) to decide a rare refusal bark path versus normal UI open.
On a rare random result, emit the refusal string "leave my bones alone... " to the player and suspend further processing.
Open the item's UI/gump (id 0x0C) on the enabled path when the rare refusal does not occur.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: DEADNS::look: When a player/actor examines the dead-body item, the item sets processing context (info/process-exclude), then emits the short description string "dead body " directed at the inspecting process and suspends. This is a short, purely descriptive 'U8P_DEADNS::look' handler.
